# Bactopia

> Bactopia is a flexible Nextflow pipeline for complete analysis of bacterial genomes. It
> processes per-isolate FASTQs through quality control, assembly, annotation, sequence typing,
> and more — then provides a suite of comparative analysis workflows (Bactopia Tools) for
> pan-genome, phylogenetics, and population-level analyses.

Bactopia is organized as a three-tier Nextflow pipeline:
- **Tier 1 — Workflows**: User-facing entry points (`workflows/`)
- **Tier 2 — Subworkflows**: Reusable orchestration components (`subworkflows/`)
- **Tier 3 — Modules**: Individual tool wrappers (`modules/`)

All components use standardized GroovyDoc documentation and static typing.

## Core Entry Points

- [main.nf](main.nf): Pipeline entry point; delegates to workflows
- [nextflow.config](nextflow.config): Top-level configuration and profile definitions
- [nextflow_schema.json](nextflow_schema.json): JSON Schema for all pipeline parameters

## Workflows (Tier 1)

- [workflows/bactopia-tools/](workflows/bactopia-tools/): Comparative analysis workflows (pan-genome, phylogenetics, typing)
- [workflows/cleanyerreads/](workflows/cleanyerreads/): Read QC and decontamination workflow
- [workflows/staphopia/](workflows/staphopia/): Staphylococcus aureus-targeted analysis workflow
- [workflows/teton/](workflows/teton/): Species identification and QC workflow

## Subworkflows (Tier 2)

- [subworkflows/bactopia/assembler/](subworkflows/bactopia/assembler/): Assembly orchestration
- [subworkflows/bactopia/gather/](subworkflows/bactopia/gather/): Input collection and preparation
- [subworkflows/bactopia/qc/](subworkflows/bactopia/qc/): Quality control orchestration
- [subworkflows/bactopia/sketcher/](subworkflows/bactopia/sketcher/): Minmer sketch orchestration

## Modules (Tier 3)

74 tool-specific modules live under `modules/`. Each module directory contains:
- `main.nf`: Process definition with GroovyDoc header
- `meta.yml`: Tool metadata, inputs, outputs, and parameter descriptions
- `tests/`: nf-test test cases

Key module categories:
- [modules/blast/](modules/blast/): BLAST suite (blastn, blastp, blastx, tblastn, tblastx)
- [modules/bakta/](modules/bakta/): Genome annotation
- [modules/mlst/](modules/mlst/): Multi-locus sequence typing
- [modules/amrfinderplus/](modules/amrfinderplus/): AMR gene detection
- [modules/snippy/](modules/snippy/): Variant calling
- [modules/prokka/](modules/prokka/): Genome annotation (legacy)

## Machine-Readable Component Index

- [data/catalog.json](data/catalog.json): Auto-generated catalog of all modules, subworkflows, and workflows with contracts (takes/emits), dependencies, tool versions, and ext requirements. Generated by `bactopia-catalog` from bactopia-py.

## AI Agent Documentation

- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Master map for AI agents — architecture, conventions, and documentation index
- [.claude/docs/standards/01-style-guide.md](.claude/docs/standards/01-style-guide.md): GroovyDoc templates and formatting rules
- [.claude/docs/standards/02-logic-rules.md](.claude/docs/standards/02-logic-rules.md): Component classification logic
- [.claude/docs/standards/03-technical-specs.md](.claude/docs/standards/03-technical-specs.md): Variable naming, types, Path? optional inputs
- [.claude/docs/standards/04-subworkflow-documentation.md](.claude/docs/standards/04-subworkflow-documentation.md): Subworkflow documentation methodology
- [.claude/docs/standards/05-module-documentation.md](.claude/docs/standards/05-module-documentation.md): Module documentation methodology
- [.claude/docs/standards/07-tier-architecture.md](.claude/docs/standards/07-tier-architecture.md): Tier architecture rules, plugin functions, ext system, catalog.json
- [.claude/docs/project/01-repository-structure.md](.claude/docs/project/01-repository-structure.md): Full directory layout
- [.claude/docs/project/02-development-workflow.md](.claude/docs/project/02-development-workflow.md): Step-by-step guide for adding new tools
- [.claude/docs/reference/01-examples.md](.claude/docs/reference/01-examples.md): Annotated implementation examples

## Key Patterns for Agents

- Module inputs: Record-typed with named parameters (e.g., `(_meta: Map, fna: Path): Record`)
- Module outputs: Single `record()` with named downstream fields + generic publishing fields
- Subworkflow outputs: emit `sample_outputs` and/or `run_outputs` with standardized record structure
- Optional file inputs use `Path?` types with null checks (e.g., `r1: Path?` is null when not provided)
- Indentation: 4 spaces in Groovy/Nextflow, 2 spaces in YAML
- Configuration for module behavior: `task.ext` properties in module.config files

- [README.md](README.md): Project overview and quick start
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md): Human contributor guide
